category_title: Marginalized and struggling urban students with histories of truancy and initial disengagement
minimal_context: The student is asked to write a history essay using their textbook and paper during a 50-minute lesson.
dialogue:
TUTOR: For today's 50-minute lesson, please use your textbook and paper to write an essay about the chapter we just read.

STUDENT: I'm just trying to get in and get out so I can graduate. I hated learning about history because of all the dates.

TUTOR: You still need to complete the assignment. Please open your textbook.

STUDENT: I never attended social studies class at my other school. Like I didn't even want to look at the book.

TUTOR: Just look at the essay prompt and try to write something down on your paper.

STUDENT: (Pushes the paper away) I look at it and I'm like, I can't write this. I'm not doing this.

options.A: Bring digital storytelling into the classroom, allowing the student to draw pictures and add their own voice to the essay.
options.B: Explicitly teach a concrete strategy and 'demystify' the history essay format from the beginning of the course.
options.C: Create a classroom writing community founded on respect for choice and individual perspectives during the 50-minute lessons.
options.D: Implement an intervention package consisting of reduced teacher reprimands and increased behavior-specific praise within a framework of student-constructed rules.
